readingOrder: Read Hp first to determine whether you may pass and the movement authority from this signal.
Read any Zs 3 for the speed applying from this signal.
Read Vr for what to expect at the next main signal.
Read any Zs 3v for the speed expected at the next signal or defined location.

pages.title: Hp 0: Stop
pages.recognise: One red light on a main signal, or two red lights on some signal-head arrangements.
do: Do not pass without a valid subsidiary indication or other authority.
pages.pzb: An active 2000 Hz magnet at the signal will cause an emergency brake application if passed without the required authority.

pages.title: Hp 1: Proceed
pages.recognise: One green light on the main-signal part.
pages.meaning: Proceed at the speed permitted by the route, timetable and any accompanying indicator.
do: Obey any Zs 3 speed indication displayed with the signal.
pages.pzb: The plain proceed aspect normally needs no acknowledgement, although nearby signs or restrictions may independently require one.

pages.title: Hp 2: Proceed slowly
pages.recognise: Green over yellow on the main-signal part.
pages.meaning: Proceed at reduced speed. The exact permitted speed depends on the applicable rules and any Zs 3 indication.
do: Reduce to the applicable speed by the signal.
Read any Zs 3 numeral rather than assuming a single universal speed.
pages.pzb: PZB response depends on the associated magnet and speed supervision arrangement.

pages.title: Vr 0: Expect stop
pages.recognise: Two yellow lights diagonally arranged on the distant-signal part.
pages.meaning: Expect the next main signal to show stop.
do: Acknowledge PZB when the associated 1000 Hz influence is active.
Brake so you can stop before the next main signal.
pages.pzb: Normally associated with a 1000 Hz influence when active.

pages.title: Vr 1: Expect proceed
pages.recognise: Two green lights diagonally arranged on the distant-signal part.
pages.meaning: Expect the next main signal to show proceed.
do: Continue at the permitted speed while observing other restrictions.
pages.pzb: Normally no acknowledgement is required for the plain clear warning.

pages.title: Vr 2: Expect proceed slowly
pages.recognise: Green and yellow lights diagonally arranged on the distant-signal part.
pages.meaning: Expect the next main signal to permit passage at reduced speed.
do: Begin braking for the restrictive speed.
Read any Zs 3v indication for the announced value.
pages.pzb: A restrictive warning may require acknowledgement when the associated 1000 Hz influence is active.
